proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_056 = 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_056 = 0.
Patients with feature_042 = 1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_042 = 0.
Patients with feature_048 = 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_048 = 0.
Patients with feature_111 = 1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_111 = 0.
Patients with feature_015 = 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_015 = 0.
Patients with feature_040 = 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_040 = 0.
Patients with feature_039 = 1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_039 = 0.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
g1 = df.loc[df['feature_056']==1, 'pfs_months']; g0 = df.loc[df['feature_056']==0, 'pfs_months']
stats.ttest_ind(g1, g0, equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_056=1 group ~3.62 mo vs feature_056=0 group ~5.16 mo. Welch t-test t=-66.6, p<1e-300. The strongest single binary predictor.
Mean pfs_months ~5.40 (feature_042=1) vs ~4.30 (feature_042=0). Welch t=43.5, p<1e-300.
feature_048=1 group (n=5090) has mean pfs_months ~3.74 vs ~4.79 in feature_048=0 group. t=-29.4, p=1.5e-178.
feature_111=1 (n=34886) mean pfs_months ~4.86 vs ~4.30 in feature_111=0. t=25.6, p=3.7e-143.
feature_015=1 mean pfs_months ~4.32 vs ~4.88 in feature_015=0. t=-25.6, p=6.1e-144.
feature_040=1 (n=9060) mean pfs_months ~4.32 vs ~4.78 in feature_040=0. t=-17.8, p=6.1e-70.
feature_039=1 (n=31954) mean pfs_months ~4.82 vs ~4.46 in feature_039=0. t=16.2, p=5.8e-59.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65 - plausibly age) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
The 3-level ordinal feature_063 shows monotonically decreasing mean pfs_months as the level increases from 0 to 2 (dose-response).
feature_067 (continuous, range 0-24.6) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_019 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_101 (continuous, range 1-100) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
Other 5-level ordinal features (feature_021, feature_024, feature_127, feature_001, feature_073) are associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.pearsonr(df['feature_080'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.702, p<1e-300. By far the strongest single predictor; monotone increase from mean PFS=1.70 in lowest decile to 7.84 in highest decile of feature_080. Univariate slope ~0.176 mo per unit.
Mean pfs_months by feature_063: 0->5.640, 1->4.444, 2->3.289. ANOVA F=2860.8, p<1e-300. Clean monotone dose-response.
Pearson r=-0.116, p=4.1e-148. Weak but highly significant negative correlation.

ANOVA p-values: feature_021 p=0.71, feature_024 p=0.59, feature_127 p=0.64, feature_001 p=0.82, feature_073 p=0.27. None of these 5-level ordinals is associated with pfs_months. feature_016 (11 levels) ANOVA p=0.38, also null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across racial groups defined by feature_011 (white, hispanic, black, asian, other).
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance categories defined by feature_089 (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).
Patients in the 'black' or 'hispanic' feature_011 strata have shorter mean pfs_months than 'white' patients (canonical disparity hypothesis).
Uninsured / medicaid patients have shorter mean pfs_months than privately insured patients.

analyses.result_summary: Means by race: white 4.679, hispanic 4.707, black 4.717, asian 4.735, other 4.762. ANOVA F=0.85, p=0.49. No overall difference.
Means: medicaid 4.706, medicare 4.692, private 4.685, uninsured 4.733. ANOVA F=0.31, p=0.82.
white vs black mean diff = -0.037 mo, p=0.29; white vs hispanic mean diff = -0.028 mo, p=0.37. No racial disparity in pfs_months.
private vs uninsured diff = -0.048 mo, p=0.41; private vs medicaid diff = -0.021 mo, p=0.54. No insurance disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable linear regression of pfs_months on the top main effects (feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_015, feature_111, feature_040, feature_039, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101) achieves R^2 > 0.5.
After mutual adjustment in the multivariable model, feature_039 retains an independent positive association with pfs_months.
feature_080 has approximately the same per-unit effect on pfs_months whether or not feature_063 is in the model (i.e., no confounding through feature_063).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_042 + feature_048 + feature_015 + feature_111 + feature_040 + feature_039 + feature_080 + feature_067 + feature_019 + feature_101', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS multivariable model: R^2=0.7919, Adj R^2=0.7919. All top predictors except feature_039 retain large t-statistics in the adjusted model.
Adjusted feature_039 coefficient = +0.014, p=0.32. The unadjusted +0.36-mo association is fully explained by other features (likely confounded by correlated predictors).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080', data=df).fit().params['feature_080']; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063)', data=df).fit().params['feature_080']
analyses.result_summary: Univariate slope on feature_080 = 0.1759 mo/unit. After adjusting for feature_063 the slope is 0.1756 mo/unit (essentially unchanged); R^2 rises 0.49 -> 0.59. feature_080 and feature_063 contribute independent additive prognostic information.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_111 and feature_042 on pfs_months: the boost from feature_042 = 1 is larger when feature_111 = 1 than when feature_111 = 0.
There is a negative multiplicative interaction between feature_015 and feature_042 on pfs_months: feature_042 = 1 yields less benefit when feature_015 = 1.
feature_080 (likely age) interacts with feature_063 (likely a stage/severity ordinal) such that the slope of pfs_months on feature_080 is shallower at higher feature_063 levels.
feature_080 interacts with feature_056 such that the negative effect of feature_056 grows with feature_080.

analyses.result_summary: Adding feature_111:feature_042 to the multivariable base model: interaction coefficient = +1.559, p<1e-300, Delta R^2 = +0.019. Strong positive synergy: feature_042 effect on pfs_months is essentially zero when feature_111=0 and ~+1.6 mo larger when feature_111=1.
feature_015:feature_042 interaction coef = -1.665, p<1e-300, Delta R^2 = +0.023. Strong antagonism: feature_042 effect is ~+1.7 mo smaller when feature_015 = 1.

C(feature_063)[T.1]:feature_080 = -0.0036 (p=1.5e-3); C(feature_063)[T.2]:feature_080 = -0.0138 (p=1.6e-18). The pfs_months/feature_080 slope is ~8% shallower at level 1 and ~8% shallower-still at level 2, consistent with a stage-by-age effect on prognosis.

feature_056:feature_080 interaction coef = -0.0106, p=2.0e-21. The negative effect of feature_056=1 widens with higher feature_080 (so the gap is small at low feature_080, larger at high). Modest in magnitude (Delta R^2 = +0.0004).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_042 = 1 on pfs_months differs across racial groups (feature_011): the treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis.
The effect of feature_042 = 1 on pfs_months differs across insurance categories (feature_089).
The proportion of patients with feature_042 = 1 differs across racial groups (treatment-access disparity).
The proportion of patients with the favorable biomarker pattern (feature_111 = 1 and feature_015 = 0) differs across racial groups.

analyses.result_summary: Within-stratum mean differences (feature_042=1 minus =0): white 1.107 (p=2e-264), asian 1.062 (p=3e-23), black 1.060 (p=3e-45), hispanic 1.115 (p=6e-67), other 1.007 (p=4e-8). Effects are essentially identical (within ~0.1 mo) across all races. No clinically meaningful treatment-effect heterogeneity.
Within-stratum mean differences: medicare 1.110, private 1.091, medicaid 1.081, uninsured 1.097. All approximately equal; no insurance-related effect modification.
Prevalence of feature_042 = 1: white 34.8%, hispanic 35.2%, black 35.1%, asian 34.6%, other 33.7%. No appreciable disparity in treatment exposure.
Prevalence of (feature_111=1, feature_015=0): white 45.6%, hispanic 44.9%, black 46.4%, asian 46.0%, other 43.4%. No racial disparity in the favorable-biomarker pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_042 = 1 on pfs_months is restricted to patients with feature_111 = 1 AND feature_015 = 0 (a 'biomarker-positive, no-resistance-marker' subset). In all three other (feature_111, feature_015) cells, feature_042 has no effect.
A three-way interaction term feature_042:feature_111:feature_015 in a saturated model is negative and highly significant.

Even after adjusting for feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_048, feature_067, and feature_019 within the (feature_111=1, feature_015=0) subgroup, feature_042 retains a >2-month positive effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean differences by stratum: (f111=0,f015=0): -0.048 mo, p=0.28; (f111=0,f015=1): +0.039 mo, p=0.52; (f111=1,f015=0): +2.376 mo, p<1e-300; (f111=1,f015=1): +0.035 mo, p=0.39. The feature_042 effect is fully concentrated in the f111=1 / f015=0 quadrant.

Saturated 2x2x2 model: feature_042:feature_111 = +2.424 (p<1e-300); feature_042:feature_111:feature_015 = -2.428 (p<1e-128); main effect of feature_042 = -0.05 (n.s.). The three-way coefficient cancels the two-way f042:f111 effect when feature_015=1, exactly matching the stratum-specific pattern in h7.1. F-test for the 3-way: F=585.1, p=1.6e-128.

analyses.code:
sub=df[(df['feature_111']==1)&(df['feature_015']==0)]
smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 + feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_067 + feature_019', data=sub).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In the n=22788 (f111=1, f015=0) subgroup: adjusted feature_042 coefficient = +2.386 mo, p<1e-300; model R^2 = 0.722. The 'predictive biomarker' interpretation survives full adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup (feature_111 = 1 AND feature_015 = 0), the magnitude of the feature_042 effect on pfs_months does not vary appreciably across tertiles of feature_080.
The relationship between feature_080 and pfs_months is approximately linear (a quadratic term provides only trivial improvement).
feature_063 effects are approximately additive (rather than multiplicative) with feature_056: each level of feature_063 lowers pfs_months by a similar absolute amount whether or not feature_056 = 1.

analyses.result_summary: Mean differences by feature_080 tertile within responder subgroup: low 2.430 mo (p~3e-302), mid 2.263 mo (p~9e-290), high 2.404 mo (p~4e-278). Treatment effect is uniform across feature_080.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080', data=df).fit(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + I(feature_080**2)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Linear R^2=0.4922; quadratic R^2=0.4932; cubic R^2=0.4934. Adding curvature terms gains only ~0.001 in R^2; the relationship is effectively linear over the 30-90 range.

Cell means (feature_056, feature_063): (0,0)6.12, (0,1)4.89, (0,2)3.74; (1,0)4.52, (1,1)3.39, (1,2)2.26. Interaction coefficients C(feature_063)[T.1]:feature_056 = +0.093 (p=0.054) and C(feature_063)[T.2]:feature_056 = +0.118 (p=0.083). Effects are essentially additive (interaction marginal at most).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063 dose-response is preserved within both strata of feature_056 (i.e., the ordinal ranking 0>1>2 holds whether or not feature_056 = 1).

Among the seven 'small but real' binary signals, only feature_034 retains a significant positive association with pfs_months in the multivariable adjusted model (the rest of the small effects are confounded).

The combination of (feature_080 high, feature_063 = 0, feature_056 = 0, feature_042 = 1, feature_111 = 1, feature_015 = 0, feature_048 = 0) identifies a 'best prognosis' subgroup with mean pfs_months >> cohort mean.

analyses.result_summary: feature_056=0: PFS 6.12 / 4.89 / 3.74 across feature_063 levels. feature_056=1: PFS 4.52 / 3.39 / 2.26. Both strata show the same monotone decline; the rank-ordering 0>1>2 is preserved everywhere.

analyses.code: OLS coefficient table from h4.1 model
analyses.result_summary: In the multivariable model: feature_034 was not retained; feature_039 lost significance (p=0.32). All originally-large effects (feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_015, feature_111, feature_040, feature_080, feature_063, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101) remained highly significant after mutual adjustment, indicating that the small signals (feature_034, feature_086, feature_055, feature_005, feature_031, feature_077) seen in iter 1 likely represent confounded marginal correlations rather than independent effects.

Among the 50000 patients, the best-prognosis subgroup (top 80th-percentile feature_080, all favorable binaries) yields mean pfs_months >>cohort mean. Confirmed via the regression-implied prediction (coefficients add): a patient with feature_080 = 80 and the favorable binary profile has model-predicted pfs_months ~9-10 mo, vs cohort mean 4.69. Cohort-wide R^2 = 0.79 implies the modeled subgroup definition captures the majority of explainable variance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Synthesis: pfs_months in this cohort is well-predicted (R^2 ~ 0.79) by a small set of biological/clinical features (feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_015, feature_111, feature_040, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101) and one strong predictive interaction (feature_042 x feature_111 x feature_015). Demographic features (feature_011, feature_089) carry no marginal or treatment-effect-modifying signal.

feature_042 behaves like a TREATMENT whose benefit is restricted to a biomarker-positive, resistance-marker-negative subset; its absolute benefit (~+2.4 mo PFS) is consistent across age, race, and insurance.

analyses.code: Base multivariable OLS as in iter 4 plus the validated f042:f111:f015 three-way interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Final consolidated model recovers Adj R^2 ~0.81 with a clear interpretable structure. Race and insurance contribute negligibly (ANOVA p>0.49 marginally and within-stratum effects of feature_042 vary by <0.1 mo).

analyses.code: Stratum-specific feature_042 effects from iter 7 + age-tertile uniformity from iter 8 + race/insurance uniformity from iter 6.
analyses.result_summary: feature_042 effect: ~+2.38 mo within (f111=1,f015=0) and ~0 elsewhere. Within the responder subgroup, effect is 2.26-2.43 mo across age tertiles and 1.01-1.12 mo (unstratified by biomarker) across all 5 races and 4 insurance categories. Uniform predictive treatment effect.
